generated_oml:
instance HokuyoUST10LXLidar : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "360° LiDAR delivering 360‑degree point clouds at 10 Hz"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudStream
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "RGB‑D camera supplying RGB‑D imagery at 30 fps"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraRGBDStream
]

instance VedderESC : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic Speed Controller reporting wheel speed and electrical current"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ESCDataStream
]

instance LiDARPointCloudStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLidar
]

instance DepthCameraRGBDStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera
]

instance ESCDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VedderESC
]

instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "3D point cloud data from LiDAR"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudStream
]

instance DepthCameraRGBDData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "RGB‑D image data from depth camera"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraRGBDStream
]

instance ESCDataPoint : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Wheel speed and electrical current data from ESC"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ESCDataStream
]

instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Clustering and tracking of objects from LiDAR point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance ACCBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control behavioral model consuming depth camera images to estimate vehicle dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCOffloadingEnabler
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that processes clustering and tracking model data"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataProcessingService
]

instance ACCOffloadingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that offloads ACC computation to remote DT"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that persists raw sensor data and processed outputs"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStorageService
]

instance RemoteCommandEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that receives remote commands and translates them into vehicle actuation"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes point cloud clustering and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC routine to remote DT and returns desired speed command"
    DTDFVocab:provides DesiredSpeedCommandAction, ACCBehaviorInsight
]

instance DataStorageService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Persists raw sensor data and model outputs for analysis and reporting"
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, and issue emergency braking"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, SetSpeedLimitAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Visualises real‑time vehicle state, projected driving path, and detected object tracks allows report generation"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, ObjectTrackInsight, PerformanceDiagnosticInsight
]

instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time telemetry of vehicle speed, heading, and status"
]

instance ObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time object tracks and bounding boxes generated from LiDAR processing"
]

instance PerformanceDiagnosticInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Diagnostics of system performance collected from reports"
]

instance ACCBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of ACC decisions based on lead‑vehicle distance"
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to enable or disable Adaptive Cruise Control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetSpeedLimitAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to set desired speed limit for ACC"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Emergency braking command issued remotely"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DesiredSpeedCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Desired speed command computed by ACC offloading routine"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands originate from the Control Panel user interface front‑end and are transmitted via the ROS 2 network to the Actuation front‑end. The Actuation component converts the desired speed, motor voltage commands, and steering assist signals into low‑level actuator signals that drive the motor actuation and steering servo actuation hardware. The Data Translation component is again employed to serialize and align the command messages. No explicit authentication, validation, feedback or conflict resolution mechanisms are described in the document."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The system enforces a global clock synchronization that guarantees a maximum skew of 100 ms between any device involved in computation offloading and data acquisition. This 100 ms bound satisfies the most stringent requirements of safety‑critical vehicular standards (many of which mandate stricter synchronisation), while remaining achievable given the hardware limitations of the 1/10‑scale vehicle platform. All inter‑component communication is therefore time‑stamped against a unified reference clock with a precision of 100 ms; no finer granularity is specified or required by the documented design."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Architecture follows the ISO 23247 reference architecture for digital twins, with functional entities (FEs) mapped to individual ROS 2 packages. Each package contains one or more ROS 2 nodes that expose specific services such as data preprocessing, point‑cloud clustering, tracking, and actuator control. Entities are logically grouped into: (1) **User Entity** – hosts the singular User Interface FE that is split into a 3D visualization node and a control‑panel node; (2) **Digital Twin Entity** – provides data management, storage, and control logic; (3) **Physical Twin Entity** – implements data pre‑processing, device communication, and automotive‑specific functions (e.g., point‑cloud clustering, tracking). Each entity is deployed on separate hardware components (compute nodes, sensor platforms, actuator controllers), forming a distributed microservices constellation. Coordination between entities is executed via ROS 2 topic, service, and action communication, with time‑synchronization established through shared clock information. Resource management is performed at the node level, with each FE encapsulating compute, sensor, and actuator resources necessary for its role. This constellation ensures that the digital twin services mirror the physical asset, maintain temporal consistency, and support computation offloading across the distributed hardware stack."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is defined as real‑time and high‑fidelity.  Network latency across the DT components is strictly bounded to a maximum of 100 ms, and all clocks are synchronised to an accuracy of 100 ms (e.g., via GPS or IEEE 1588 Time‑Protocol).  Validation and verification of models are performed by cross‑comparing model predictions with live sensor streams, with any discrepancy triggering error‑detection, correction routines, and uncertainty quantification.  Quality assurance processes include periodic sensor calibration, audit trails for data provenance, and rigorous testing of data‑storage reliability."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The system’s internal software components communicate through a ROS 2 middleware stack, which routes message packets between sensors, processing modules, and user interfaces. External communication between the vehicle and off‑board systems is carried over a Wi‑Fi network using UDP as the protocol at the transport layer. The document does not specify bandwidth, latency, reliability, or security requirements for these links."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Vehicle‑side (edge) compute hosts the on‑board sensors, the Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking component, the Actuation front‑end, and the on‑board Data Monitoring UI. External storage and reporting are performed by a device outside the vehicle, which receives data from the Vehicle via the Wi‑Fi link and persists them using a SQL database as indicated. The architecture is therefore a hybrid edge‑to‑cloud deployment, with real‑time processing on the vehicle and aggregated data logging and reporting off‑board, but the document does not detail cloud‑specific hosting or scalability provisions."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system conforms to ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing, specifically Part 2 (Reference Architecture). ISO 23247 outlines the expected entities and functional entities required, providing a compliance framework for digital twin realization. Additionally, the reference to OPC Unified Architecture indicates adherence to the OPC UA standard for secure and interoperable data exchange between the Digital Twin Entity, Data Collection/Device Control Entity, and User Entity. No further certification or interoperability standards are explicitly mentioned."
]
